title: RFP 2026 Rec Center Roof Replacement Project
summary: REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS 2026 Recreation Center Roof Replacement Project Sealed proposals for furnishing the following to the City of Cortez, Colorado will be received by the General Services Director, City Service Center, 110 West Progress Circle, Cortez, Colorado 81321, until 3:00 P.M., on Wednesday, September 9, 2026 , at which time proposals will be publicly opened and read. The City of Cortez is soliciting bids for roofing improvements on the Cortez Recreation Center Building, in accordance with the plans and specifications. Bidders shall submit pricing to remove the damaged roofing and dispose of materials; inspect the roof deck and replace deteriorated substrate; install new insulation board, a fully adhered membrane system, and new edge metal, termination bars, and flashing; replace penetrations, boots, drains, and equipment flashings; and complete waterproofing, sealing, and final inspection. Proposal shall include a 20-year (NDL) Manufacturer Warranty?and shall consist of new coverboard, tapered insulation (minimum R-30 equivalent), 60-mil TPO membrane or approved equivalent product, coping cap metal, modify existing roof drain assemblies as necessary to restore positive drainage, new parapet caps, new edge flashing, and all manufacturer-required sealants and accessories necessary to complete the work. A MANDATORY MEETING and project site visit will be held for all interested vendors on Tuesday, August 4, 2026, at 9:30 AM and also on Thursday, August 6, at 9:30 AM at the Recreation Center. Interested vendors can choose which meeting to attend. Proposals shall be submitted in sealed envelopes, and marked on the outside with “PROPOSAL: 2026 Recreation Center Roof Replacement Project” and with the submitting Company’s name. Proposals shall be submitted in writing and signed by the proposer or its duly-authorized agent. The City of Cortez reserves the right to waive any formality or any informality in the process of awarding a bid. The City of Cortez reserves the right to accept any proposal, in whole or in part, and to reject any or all proposals if it be deemed in the best interest of the City to do so. RFP - REC CENTER ROOF REPLACEMENT PROJECT

title: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Software-as-a Service (SaaS) Solution
summary: The purpose of this Request for Proposal (RFP) is to solicit proposals from qualified vendors for a modern, cloud-based Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) solution to support RTD’s core business functions. This procurement is focused on the selection of the ERP software platform and its capabilities to meet RTD’s business needs. As mentioned earlier, there will be a separate and follow-on RFP for the chosen ERP Systems Integrator (SI) services. So, please note that this RFP is for ERP SaaS software providers only. This RFP has two key objectives: 1) the primary objective is to identify an ERP solution to replace RTD’s existing Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) across finance, procurement, and inventory management; and 2) the secondary objective is to invite the same ERP solution proposer to also propose its solution for human resources business functions. The RTD is seeking this dual objective strategy to “eventually” align with its strategic goal to be served by one-single ERP solution for all of its administrative business needs. In support of this RFP, RTD has developed detailed, agency-specific business and functional requirements for the finance, procurement, and inventory management functions; and for human resources (HR), RTD is using a set of representative HR requirements that are reflective of public transit agency HR business needs. Evaluation will focus on finance, procurement and inventory requirements, along with how the proposer brings in the HR modules/functionality to meet and align with RTD’s long-term vision of a “single-ERP platform” for all of its business needs. RTD will review proposals for alignment with requirements, long-term strategic fit, and overall value; including the maturity, flexibility, and extensibility of the proposed platform. RTD is interested in solutions that not only meet current needs but demonstrate the ability to support evolving business requirements, enable seamless integration across all ERP and other functional domains, and drive continuous improvement in business processes and data management. RTD encourages participation from all qualified ERP vendors and is committed to a fair, open, and competitive procurement process. RTD will select the solution that best meets its overall business needs and long-term strategic objectives.

title: Computer Hardware Procurement & Extended Warranty
summary: REQUEST FOR QUOTATION Rugged Laptop Computers -- Patrol Vehicle & Field Operations Clear Creek County Sheriff's Office, Georgetown, Colorado Issuing Department: Clear Creek County Sheriff's Office / IT Department RFQ Number: RFQ #IT-2026-004 Issue Date: July 14, 2026 Submission Deadline: July 22, 2026 at 5:00 PM MDT Submission Method: Email only -- see Section 5 for instructions Contact / Submit To: For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. Award Basis: Best overall value -- price is one factor among several 1. Purpose and Overview The Clear Creek County Sheriff's Office, in coordination with the Clear Creek County IT Department, is soliciting competitive quotations from qualified vendors for the procurement of rugged laptop computers for use in patrol vehicles and field operations. This is an open public request; any qualified vendor may respond. The Sheriff's Office requires high-durability, fully-featured mobile computing platforms suitable for law enforcement patrol environments, including in-vehicle use, outdoor field work, and conditions common to mountain and rural terrain. title: Wayfinding Signage for Clear Creek Trail
summary: The Clear Creek Trail and Wheat Ridge Greenbelt Trail Signage Guidelines project is the culmination of a years-long planning process that involved the several jurisdictional entities and the consultant team. A concrete trail adjacent to Clear Creek currently runs from the west side of Golden in Jefferson County to the South Platte River Trail (SPRT) in Adams County. It is primarily an off-street trail, but there are some sections that are on-street and there are a few areas where the trail is not continuous or it does not appear continuous due to the lack of signage and wayfinding. Along this extent, the trail travels through four counties, eleven cities and towns, three recreational districts, and a state wildlife area. These jurisdictions identify this trail with various names. It is known as the Clear Creek Trail in Adams County, the City of Golden, and the City and County of Denver; the Clear Creek County Greenway in Clear Creek County; the Clear Creek Canyon Trail in Jefferson County; and the Wheat Ridge Greenbelt in the City of Wheat Ridge. The desire to create a singular name and identity for this trail arose. When built to the fullest extent, the Clear Creek Trail will be part of a trail system from Glenwood Springs to the Denver International Airport.

title: Harvest Grass Hay on County-Owned Property
summary: Request for Proposal Harvest Grass Hay on County-Owned Property The Board of County Commissioners intends to contract with a vendor/operator for a three (3) year period with annual renewal to harvest grass hay on county property known as the Deer Creek Meadow. It is estimated that approximately 15-30 tons of hay will be harvested. All bids will be based on a price per ton for all hay harvested and should reflect a fair market value at the time the hay is harvested. BID PREPARATION Interested parties should submit one (1) copy of their bid, no later than October 1, 2024, to the attention of Mike Smith, Interim County Manager. Submittals may be mailed to PO Box 1373, Fairplay, CO 80440, or emailed to For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. . Submittals should contain a cover letter, not exceeding two pages, and concise, informative responses to each of the selection criteria listed below. Other information may be included, as well. EVALUATION CRITERIA The Commissioners will evaluate each bid based on the following factors: - General background of bidding party. - Experience with similar operation contracts. Include references. - What estimated time frame will all the hay be harvested and removed from the property. SELECTION PROCEDURE Based on the information contained in the bid, an interview committee will determine which party could best meet the needs of the County. The interview committee will initiate negotiations with the successful party and make a recommendation to the Board of County Commissioners for approval. The County reserves the right to reject any or all proposals received. Questions regarding this request for proposal (RFP) should be directed to Stevie Wilcox, For security reasons, you must enable JavaScript to view this E-mail address. , or 719-836-4245.
